reasoning: Hallucinations are fundamentally intertwined with how Large Language Models generate text probabilistically. While mitigation strategies (like RAG, better reinforcement learning, and fact-checking layers) will significantly reduce the frequency of hallucinations by the end of 2025, the problem will absolutely not be entirely "fixed" or "solved" in all contexts. The resolution criteria explicitly state: "If the outstanding hallucination problem still exists, this market will resolve NO." Given the high bar required for a YES resolution (zero or negligible hallucinations) and the inherent architecture of current generative AI, there will undoubtedly still be documented instances of hallucinations at the end of 2025, leading to a NO resolution.
